- Noisy toilets

### Saniflo
- Saniflo macerator specialist
- Well-stocked van with common Saniflo parts and spare machines
- Labour-only service with access to discounted Saniflo supply through a trusted partner

## How a Saniflo works

A Saniflo macerator grinds toilet waste and paper into fine slurry and pumps it through small-diameter pipework to the soil stack — useful where gravity drainage is impractical (basements, loft conversions, extensions).

Step-by-step:
1. Waste enters the macerator chamber after flushing.
2. A pressure membrane activates the microswitch and starts the motor.
3. The blade/impeller macerates solids rapidly.
4. The pump discharges slurry through the outlet and discharge line.
5. A non-return valve closes to prevent backflow when the cycle ends.

## Common Saniflo repairs

- Clearing blade obstructions (wipes, floss, foreign material)
- Clearing blocked discharge runs and correcting poor pipe routing
- Replacing a worn rubber membrane (delayed or unreliable triggering)
- Replacing non-return valves (backflow or random cycling)
- Resolving repeated pump cycling caused by slow cistern drips

## Saniflo maintenance advice

- Use only human waste and standard toilet paper
- Avoid wipes, sanitary products, cotton buds, oils and harsh acid cleaners
- Descale regularly to reduce limescale on the membrane and internals
- In hard-water areas, monthly descaling is recommended

## FAQs

**How does a Saniflo macerator work?**
A pressure membrane and microswitch trigger the motor when water rises in the chamber. The blade macerates waste into slurry which is pumped through small discharge pipework to the soil stack.

**What are the most common Saniflo repairs?**
Clearing blockages at the blade or discharge pipe, replacing worn rubber membranes, fixing non-return valves, and addressing repeated cycling caused by slow cistern leaks.

**How often should a Saniflo be descaled?**
In hard-water areas, monthly descaling is usually recommended.

**Can a Saniflo pump uphill?**
Yes. Saniflo systems pump wastewater vertically and horizontally through small pipework.

**Why is my Saniflo running when no one flushes?**
Usually a slow cistern leak or dripping inlet keeps raising the water level and triggering the membrane switch.

**How should discharge pipework be installed?**
Rigid pipework with vertical lift taken first near the unit, then a correctly-falled horizontal run with access for maintenance.

## About Craig West

Originally working in IT, Craig became a qualified plumber in 1996. He holds Gas Safe and Part P Electrical certifications. He ran Small Job On Time Same Day Plumbers Ltd, which accumulated over 1,000 reviews on Checkatrade. In 2020 he returned to IT and moved into AI at CraigWestAI.com. In 2026 he returned to plumbing.
